blocks.body: Future Simple: Forms and Use — will + V1 for spontaneous decisions, promises, offers, and predictions.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"In Future Simple, use will + V1. The form is the same with every subject.","rules":[{"label":"All subjects + will","color":"emerald","text":"Use will after every subject.","example":"I will call. / They will arrive."},{"label":"Base verb","color":"blue","text":"After will, always use V1 without to.","example":"She will help. / He will be ready."},{"label":"Short form","color":"amber","text":"In speech, will is often shortened to 'll.","example":"I'll call. / We'll see."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic formula","intro":"One pattern works with every subject.","items":[{"label":"I / you","title":"will + V1","subtitle":"I will work. / You will understand."},{"label":"he / she / it","title":"will + V1","subtitle":"She will call. / It will rain."},{"label":"we / they","title":"will + V1","subtitle":"We will help. / They will come."},{"label":"Contractions","title":"I'll / you'll / he'll / we'll","subtitle":"The short form is common in speech and informal writing."}]}

{"title":"2. When we use Future Simple","sections":[{"label":"Spontaneous decision","color":"emerald","description":"The decision is made while speaking.","examples":[{"en":"It's cold. I'll close the window.","ua":"A decision made now."},{"en":"We need milk. I'll buy some.","ua":"A spontaneous reaction."}]},{"label":"Promise or offer","color":"blue","description":"Use will for promises and offers of help.","examples":[{"en":"I'll never forget you.","ua":"A promise."},{"en":"I'll carry that bag for you.","ua":"An offer."}]},{"label":"Opinion or prediction","color":"amber","description":"Will often follows I think, probably, or perhaps.","examples":[{"en":"I think she will pass the exam.","ua":"An opinion."},{"en":"It will probably rain later.","ua":"A prediction."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Adding to after will.","wrong":"❌ She will to call.","right":"✅ She will call."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Changing the verb after will.","wrong":"❌ He will works.","right":"✅ He will work."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Ignoring the difference between will and an existing plan.","wrong":"❌ Every future idea automatically uses will.","right":"✅ Use be going to for an existing plan."}]}

{"title":"4. Short summary","items":["Affirmative = subject + will + V1.","Do not use to or change the verb after will.","The contraction 'll works with every subject.","Main uses: decisions made now, promises, offers, opinions, and predictions."]}

{"title":"5. Practice","select_title":"Exercise 1. Choose the correct form","select_intro":"Choose the correct Future Simple sentence.","selects":[{"label":"a) She will call later. / b) She will calls later.","prompt":"Which option is correct?","answer":"a"},{"label":"a) I'll help you. / b) I will to help you.","prompt":"Which option is correct?","answer":"a"}],"options":["a","b"],"input_title":"Exercise 2. Build the sentence","input_intro":"Put the words in the correct order.","inputs":[{"before":"tomorrow / call / I / you / will","after":"→","answer":"I will call you tomorrow."},{"before":"probably / rain / it / will","after":"→","answer":"It will probably rain."}]}

{"title":"Future Simple lessons","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/en/theory/future-simple/future-simple-forms","current":true},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/en/theory/future-simple/future-simple-negatives","current":false},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/en/theory/future-simple/future-simple-questions","current":false},{"label":"Time Expressions","url":"/en/theory/future-simple/future-simple-time-expressions","current":false},{"label":"Will vs Be Going To","url":"/en/theory/future-simple/will-vs-be-going-to","current":false}]}
